title: gavilea litoralis
intro: Gavilea litoralis, also known as the coastal gavilea or the coastal gilia, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Gavilea litoralis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Gavilea litoralis can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
